examples.title: Computing Steady-State Distribution
examples.description: Algorithm to find stationary distribution using Gauss-Jordan elimination
examples.code:
ALGORITHM SteadyStateDistribution
INPUT: Transition matrix P (n×n)
OUTPUT: Stationary distribution π

BEGIN
  // Create augmented matrix [P^T - I | 0]
  // We solve πP = π, which means (P^T - I)π = 0
  // Add constraint Σπ_i = 1
  
  CREATE matrix A of size n × (n+1)
  FOR i = 0 to n-1 DO
    FOR j = 0 to n-1 DO
      A[i][j] = P[j][i]  // Transpose
    END FOR
    A[i][i] = A[i][i] - 1  // Subtract 1 from diagonal
    A[i][n] = 0  // Last column for constants
  END FOR
  
  // Add constraint: sum of all π_i = 1
  FOR j = 0 to n-1 DO
    A[n-1][j] = 1
  END FOR
  A[n-1][n] = 1
  
  // Apply Gauss-Jordan elimination
  FOR k = 0 to n-1 DO
    // Find pivot
    pivot = A[k][k]
    FOR j = 0 to n DO
      A[k][j] = A[k][j] / pivot
    END FOR
    
    FOR i = 0 to n-1 DO
      IF i ≠ k THEN
        factor = A[i][k]
        FOR j = 0 to n DO
          A[i][j] = A[i][j] - factor * A[k][j]
        END FOR
      END IF
    END FOR
  END FOR
  
  // Extract solution
  FOR i = 0 to n-1 DO
    π[i] = A[i][n]
  END FOR
  
  RETURN π
END
examples.explanation: This algorithm solves the system of linear equations π = πP (i.e., π(P - I) = 0) along with the constraint Σπ_i = 1 to find the unique stationary distribution for an irreducible Markov chain.
